108.status: Made a rehab start in a Minor League game in Arizona but exited with hamstring discomfort after throwing two pitches on April 13. He's being evaluated further.

Sidelined due to a bout with mononucleosis diagnosed on Jan. 31, which delayed his start to Spring Training. Optioned to Triple-A Salt Lake on March 16 to build up stamina, will join the rotation in the coming days.

Right middle fingernail fell off after March 17 start. Threw to hitters on April 11 and “came out really good," per manager Kurt Suzuki. Has progressed to throwing bullpens with all of his pitches at the club's complex in Arizona.
Expected to pitch with High-A Rancho Cucamonga soon, per manager Kurt Suzuki. Threw a BP session on April 8.
Threw bullpen session off a mound on April 8. Played a “heavier catch” Friday in Cincinnati, per manager Kurt Suzuki. Continues to throw from 120 feet to build up arm strength.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.

Progressing well in throwing program. Yet to throw bullpens at 100 percent intensity but has been throwing without any issues. Manager Kurt Suzuki said he looks good but doesn't know when he'll face hitters just yet.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.

Played 11 rehab games for Triple-A Norfolk from March 27-April 12, going 7-for-42 (.167) with two doubles and three RBIs. Not expected to play for O's during week of April 13, will likely need more time.
Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing vs. Rangers. Went on IL five days later, then transferred to 60-day IL on April 13.
Missed April 10 and 11 games vs. Giants after dealing with extreme dehydration, which caused him to faint on April 9 off-day.
Scratched from lineup prior to April 11 game vs. Giants, then went on IL prior to the start of the contest. MRI showed encouraging results. Not expected to miss extended time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Currently on rehab assignment with Triple-A Norfolk. Allowed one run in 2 2/3 innings with five strikeouts and one walk in three outings from April 1-8.
After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Swinging a bat as of April 1. Scheduled to begin throwing progression week of April 6.
Hospitalized in Pittsburgh during April 3-5 series vs. the Pirates. Received IV antibiotics to help with swelling in left foot.
Felt discomfort while warming up in bullpen on March 21. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Playing catch and running as of March 29.
Sustained injury during March 19 Grapefruit League game. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Placed on IL to begin season.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return yet.

111.status: Plans to make next rehab start with Triple-A Worcester on April 14.
Gave up five runs while striking out five and walking one in his first rehab outing at Triple-A Worcester on April 11.
Underwent MRI imaging to compare injury to surgery from a few years ago, shut down from baseball activities for now.
Injury not considered serious, will resume throwing program soon.
Consultation with Dr. Keith Meister revealed right flexor strain. Shut down from throwing for six weeks.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Had bullpen session on April 12, per manager Craig Counsell. Will have a rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa (day TBD) before likely activation next week during home Phillies series.
Last pitched on April 7 and experienced a velo drop. Has continued throwing. Manager Craig Counsell said Maton will likely have a bullpen session next week.
Did not recover well after each of the past two outings (April 3 and April 8), per manager Craig Counsell. Timeline for return unclear.
Will undergo season-ending right elbow surgery, manager Craig Counsell announced on April 7.
Shut down from throwing on March 8. After a period of rest and rehab, resumed playing catch on April 4. Still "weeks" away, per manager Craig Counsell.
Resumed playing catch on March 3 after 10-day shutdown. Moved back to mound March 16 and was facing hitters as of April 5. Still likely "weeks" away, per manager Craig Counsell.

Medically cleared Feb. 28 by Dr. Keith Meister, who performed Steele's elbow surgery (left ulnar collateral ligament revision repair) on April 18, 2025. Has advanced to facing hitters in live BP sessions.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Cubs manager Craig Counsell said Austin could miss "months" while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Underwent an epidural injection to get relief and began light throwing on April 11. Was scheduled to hit off a tee on April 12. If that goes well, could begin taking batting practice on April 14.
Lodolo had a 10-pitch bullpen session on April 9 that wasn't at full intensity but went well. Has another bullpen session scheduled for April 12.
Bullpen session on April 9 went well and is scheduled for another one April 12. Ferguson could face some hitters on April 14 if his progression goes well.
Had arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from elbow. Shut down from throwing for six weeks; expected to miss 14-16 weeks.

114.status: Suffered injury running to second base while hitting a double in fifth inning on April 6. Imaging revealed moderate strain. Expected to miss four to eight weeks.
Made first appearance of rehab assignment on April 7 with Triple-A Columbus

115.status: After a 21-pitch bullpen session on April 12, Quintana declared himself ready to return to the rotation on April 15 at Houston.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continues playing catch at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz.
Facing hitters at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz., and is targeting the middle of April to return to Minor League action.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Threw a higher-intensity bullpen session on April 11. Will likely throw a rehab start or simulated game before being reinstated. Will not be reinstated when eligible on April 16.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Had a nasty collision with Riley Greene in outfield on April 9. Will undergo further testing to determine if surgery is needed on arm. Received five stitches to repair a laceration in mouth.
Resumed throwing program on April 9 following a scheduled rest period as part of his rehab. Played catch out to 120 feet over the past few weeks.
Received PRP injection on April 9 to address shoulder soreness after throwing during the week of April 1.
Tossed two-thirds of an inning with an unearned run on a hit along with a strikeout in a rehab appearance for Single-A Lakeland on April 7. Scheduled to make another appearance on April 11.
Threw a bullpen session on April 10.
Threw a bullpen session on April 10.
Placed on 7-day injured list at Triple-A Toledo on April 5.
Underwent surgery on Feb. 2, after offseason rehab program was unsuccessful.

117.status: Was removed from the April 11 game against the Mariners for a pinch-runner after his knee tightened up running the bases. Was scheduled to undergo imaging April 12 to determine the extent of the injury.
Flew from Seattle to Houston on April 11, one day after he walked four batters and gave up three runs in one-third of an inning in a start in Seattle.

Threw 14 pitches in a scoreless inning in his first Minor League rehab outing at Triple-A Sugar Land on April 11, walking one and striking out two. He topped out at 94.9 mph and he generated two swings and misses on his slider..
Exited April 8 start against Rockies prior to the second inning with right shoulder tightness and will be evaluated in two weeks.
Exited game against Rockies on April 8 mid-plate appearance after a swing.
Will “refrain from throwing for a few weeks,” according to the club, and will be reevaluated in two weeks. Manager Joe Espada said that the issue is muscular, with no evidence of damage to ligaments.
Threw live batting practice April 10 in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Shut down in early February. Is throwing bullpens and could face hitters during the week of April 13.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Has been battling valgus extension overload (VEO) in left elbow. Threw second bullpen April 11 and is scheduled to go on rehab assignment April 15 with Triple-A Omaha.
Slated to pitch four-plus innings for Triple-A Omaha on April 12. Has been building up in Arizona since suffering strain in late February.
Hit by a comebacker March 28 in Atlanta. Scheduled for first bullpen April 9 but fell ill and was pushed back.
Suffered minor setback while in extended spring camp, but began throwing again week of April 6. Scheduled for a bullpen session April 11.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Felt discomfort in his shoulder after his outing on April 12. The team will know more about his timeline after he undergoes an MRI.
Faced live hitters in a 15-pitch simulated inning April 11. He is expected to reach a four-inning workload before starting a rehab assignment.
Participating in defensive drills, but there is no timeline for him to resume swinging a bat. He is symptom-free.
Has continued to face hitters at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona. He is nearing a rehab assignment.
Throwing bullpen sessions at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in his shoulder. Has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training ramp-up ahead.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Has not thrown since March 13, when he tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen before a spring start. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Progressed to throwing off the mound by the time the Dodgers broke camp, but the team is building him up slowly.
Started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Pointed to left elbow and exited game on April 12 at Milwaukee. Previously underwent Tommy John surgery in 2024.
Henry allowed two runs without recording an out against the Brewers on April 12, was placed on the IL the next day.
Continuing forearm strengthening and stability work, not throwing. Nats exercising caution because Gray is returning from Tommy John surgery.
Progressed to throwing bullpen sessions at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Threw a 15-pitch bullpen session of fastballs at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla., on April 10.

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 75)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing throwing progressing, with flat ground throwing three times a week.

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 51)
120.status: Continuing throwing progression, including throwing on flat ground three times a week.

121.status: Strained his calf running first to third on April 3 in San Francisco. Expected to miss 2-3 weeks but was still not running as of April 13. Could undergo a second MRI if things don't improve.
Is about a month behind other Mets pitchers. Began a Minor League rehab assignment April 9 with Single-A St. Lucie, putting him on track to return May 9 at the latest.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Hit on the left hand with a pitch from Nathan Eovaldi on April 13. Manager Mark Kotsay said postgame that Muncy may be out April 14, but not much longer.

After originally rehabbing from a lumbar spine strain that sidelined him through Spring Training, Hoglund experienced symptoms related to a left hip impingement and is shut down while undergoing further evaluation.

134.status: Set to throw a bullpen session in Pittsburgh on April 14.

General manager Ben Cherington didn't provide a specific timeline for Triolo when asked of his return but said it would be a matter of weeks, not days. Triolo was injured while running the bases in the team’s home-opener on April 3.

135.status: Velocity was down through seven outings this season. Landed on the IL after feeling what he described as "tightness" in his elbow while playing catch on April 10. Shut down from throwing, with no timetable.
Missed World Baseball Classic due to the injury, then began the season on the IL. Began rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso April 4 and has made two scoreless appearances.
Pitched 2 1/3 innings for Triple-A El Paso on April 4, his first game action since June 2025. Expected to require a lengthy buildup, akin to a full Spring Training.
Wasn't built up enough to make the Opening Day roster. Has made scoreless starts of three and four innings, respectively, with Triple-A El Paso while on a rehab assignment.
Sustained the injury during the offseason, then aggravated it during a game on March 5. Began a rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso on March 27.
Made one spring start, but experienced soreness afterward and was sidelined. Has resumed playing catch, but has yet to throw off a mound.
Only appeared in five Cactus League games after sustaining the injury in early March. By the end of camp, had begun doing light drills.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Expected to rejoin Mariners in Seattle for in-person consultation as early as homestand beginning on April 17, and typically, these visits are a precursor to a Minors rehab assignment -- which will likely be multiple starts to build workload.

Has begun light baseball activity but not to the point of throwing, which will be the most demanding part of his recovery. Suffered injury while throwing to the bases during outfielders’ drills before the April 7 game at Texas.
Transferred to 60-day IL on April 8, which will push his recovery to late May at earliest. Suffered injury during catch play leading up to Opening Day.
Hurt his calf playing for Team Italy at World Baseball Classic. Wasn't ready to return to play by Opening Day
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Exited in the fifth inning of a 6-2 loss to the Orioles on April 11 after he was kicked in the hand/wrist area an inning earlier by a baserunner. Not in the lineup on April 12, with questionable availability off the bench.
Was added to Single-A San Jose’s roster on April 11 to begin a rehab assignment.
Struck out two over a scoreless inning in his first rehab appearance with Triple-A Sacramento on April 7.
Began a throwing program on April 6 in Arizona. Currently throwing off flat ground.
Diagnosed with a blood clot in his right arm and underwent surgery to have it removed on April 3. Expected to miss two to four months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Started a bullpen progression on Feb. 10 and is throwing twice per week.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Was slated to begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Will be sent on a rehab assignment on April 14; expected to pitch in a Minor League game on April 15.
Made his second rehab start for Triple-A Memphis on April 7, striking out seven batters over 5 1/3 innings of one-run baseball at Charlotte.
Underwent surgery last October to address Haglund’s deformities in both heels. Started his running progression on March 20, but no date has been set for a return to the lineup.

139.status: Felt good facing hitters in live BP at Tropicana Field on April 10, then threw a 25-pitch bullpen on April 12. Team still gathering information on how he feels, so he is day to day.
Rolled ankle running to first base as the DH for Triple-A Durham on April 11. Not expected to be a major issue, but was still due for more rehab games. "He just needs reps," manager Kevin Cash said.
Imaging revealed no structural damage, and manager Kevin Cash called the IL move cautionary.

Began a rehab assignment with Double-A Montgomery on April 7 and threw a scoreless inning with one strikeout. Scheduled to pitch again on April 10 and April 12, then should transfer rehab to Triple-A Durham. Will get a full Spring Training-type workload before joining Rays.
Has resumed playing catch and is doing well. “I'd like to be optimistic right now that he'll be back shorter than later,” manager Kevin Cash said.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July. Playing catch with no issues.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Will resume throwing in early April, but will need a while to build up after being shut down so long.

140.status: Ran the bases, hit in the cages and shagged in the outfield pregame April 13. Could be able to play in the coming days.
Experienced "not a setback, but he's pushed back," per manager Skip Schumaker. Threw 27 pitches in a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 2. Has not pitched since.
Suffered an intercostal strain during a perfect inning of relief on April 4. Manager Skip Schumaker said he doesn’t expect the IL stint to last too long.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Dealing with minor back spasms, but not serious enough for an IL stint, which is why Brandon Valenzuela has played three straight.
Threw 52 pitches Thursday in Single-A and will make another rehab start next week, likely Tuesday or Wednesday. This could be his final rehab start needed.
Will throw off the mound for the third time on Saturday as he continues to build up.
Expected to move into Minor League rehab games at some point next week, likely targeting 50 pitches.
Throwing another side session this weekend at the club's complex in Dunedin and is nearing rehab games.
Underwent surgery on April 7 and had a screw inserted in his thumb. Expectation is a six-week recovery.
Will undergo surgery on April 15 and is expected to miss six months, which likely ends his season.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

Was scheduled to begin throwing again March 10, which is a step in the right direction after being shut down the past couple of weeks. The Blue Jays were working to find some preventative measures to help Tiedemann avoid further elbow soreness in the future.

142.status: Twisted a cleat and needed attention from the trainer on April 9 but stayed in the game. Scratched from the lineup on April 10 and underwent an MRI that revealed the sprain.
Laweryson was unavailable April 10 versus the Blue Jays due to right forearm soreness and was placed on the IL.
Threw off a mound for the first time on April 7. Still at least six weeks away from returning.
Threw live batting practice on April 8. Will most likely throw one more time before moving to a rehab assignment.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.More >

143.playerName: Aidan Miller (Phillies' No. 1 prospect, No. 20 overall)
143.status: Miller has started to "increase his activity" with rotational exercises, but there is no timetable for his return. As previously reported, Miller has not started to swing a bat.
Bowlan, 29, last pitched on April 12, allowing a walk and a single with two strikeouts in the eighth inning of the Phillies' 4-3 loss to Arizona.
He is throwing bullpen sessions in Clearwater, Fla. Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique.

Struck out six over 4 1/3 innings in a third rehab start on April 8. Scheduled to make two more rehab starts for Double-A Reading on April 14 and April 19. If he makes both, could rejoin the Phillies’ rotation in Atlanta on April 24-26.

144.status: Touched 95 mph in a live batting practice on April 11. Will begin a rehab assignment April 16. Expected to make three rehab starts before rejoining Atlanta's rotation.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
A family matter delayed the start of his rehab assignment. His assignment will now begin on April 14.

144.injury: Torn tendon in right middle finger
144.status: Still hasn't faced live pitching, but the Braves are hoping he could begin a rehab assignment within the next couple weeks.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow. Could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Cannon was called up from Triple-A Charlotte for bulk work in the series finale against the Royals on April 12. He faced three hitters, walked all three and then left due to injury.
Set for workout with big league team in Kansas City ahead of rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte starting on April 12.
Murphy pitched in six games as of April 9 and hadn't been able to recover, according to manager Will Venable, who also said the decision to go on the IL was made quickly.
Hit off a tee for the past three days and felt no pain where it was grabbing before. There’s still no set date for his Minor League injury rehabilitation assignment.
Manager Will Venable listed the injury, suffered on April 6, as a Grade 1 strain when talking on April 7. Hays will be out of action for 2 to 4 weeks.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but he was with the team for the home opener. Transferred to the 60-day IL on April 7.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.

146.playerName: Aiva Arquette (Marlins' No. 4 prospect, No. 44 overall)
146.status: Underwent core muscle surgery on Feb. 27. Sustained left groin injury during offseason workouts. Rested before arriving in Jupiter, where it flared up again.

Played second rehab game with Triple-A Jacksonville April 12, getting three at-bats and playing eight innings in left field. Began rehab assignment April 10. Felt tightness on March 20 rounding third. Missed 1-2 weeks of spring with a strain of the same hamstring.
Will undergo left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13. Placed on IL on April 10. Felt discomfort after making diving attempt during Marlins' win over Reds on April 9.
Rehabbing in Jupiter, Fla. Initial timeline of 4-6 weeks after being placed on IL on March 28 (retroactive to March 25).

According to manager Clayton McCullough, further ahead than Morel and performing baseball activities in Jupiter. Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). Initial 6-8 week timeline from March 22.

Placed on 10-day injured list on March 25 (retroactive to March 22). Imaging revealed severity after being scratched from March 11 lineup. Stretched his side making a backhanded play during individual pregame work that day.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Placed on 60-day injured list on March 25. Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 12. Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Threw a live batting practice session on April 13 -- tossing three innings, fifty pitches. He'll throw again in five days, either in another live session or in a rehab game.
Tossed a live batting practice session on April 13 of three innings and 42 pitches. Cole said he's on track to throw again in five days, has no complaints regarding stamina and feel for his pitches.
Scheduled to begin a Minor League rehab assignment with Double-A Somerset on April 14. Goal is to log about 55 at-bats before MLB activation, and play shortstop consistently.
Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

158.status: Was undergoing additional tests after getting hurt on April 12, but manager Pat Murphy said, "We're most likely to get some bad news." (last updated: April 12)

Struck in the kneecap by first baseman Gary Sánchez's errant throw on April 11 and had an MRI scan to rule out serious injury. Manager Pat Murphy said those results were encouraging. (last updated: April 12)

Tweaked his right knee during a relief outing against the Nationals on April 11 and reported feeling "perfectly fine" the next morning. If that remains the case, he's expected to rejoin the rotation against the Blue Jays. (last updated: April 12)
With two live batting practice sessions in the books, was scheduled to face hitters next in extended spring training on April 16. (last updated: April 12)
Follow-up imaging on April 9 showed healing, but Chourio was not yet cleared to resume hitting. (last updated: April 10)
Had his stitches out on April 9 and will need several more weeks of healing before he can resume hitting. (last updated April 10)

Returned from his rehab assignment on April 4 after suffering a rib-cage injury while coming back from a shoulder injury. The setback is expected to delay his comeback by several weeks. (last updated: April 7)
Initial hope is that he can avoid surgery and recover with treatment. (last updated: April 7)
First live batting practice session was scheduled for April 8. (last updated: April 7)
Injured running to first base on March 9, and an MRI revealed a more serious injury than initially believed. (last updated: March 25)
